content:
# Dorea v2 Implementation Plan — Issue #24

## GitHub Issue
chunzhe10/dorea-workspace#24 — "Dorea v2: Rust+PyTorch pipeline rewrite for 4K video"

## Phase 1: Cargo Workspace + Core Pipeline (Rust)

### Crate: dorea-cli
- Binary entry point
- CLI: `dorea grade --input clip.mp4 --output graded.mp4 [--config dorea.toml]`
- CLI: `dorea calibrate --keyframes ./kf/ --depth ./depth/ --output cal.dorea-cal`
- Orchestrates the full pipeline

### Crate: dorea-core
Files to create:
- `src/lut.rs` — Depth-stratified LUT building
  - Port from `working/sea_thru_poc/run_fixed_hsl_lut_poc.py` function `build_fixed_depth_luts()`
  - 5 depth zones × 33³ grid, importance-weighted pixel binning
  - NN fill via KD-tree for empty cells
  - NO Gaussian smoothing (σ=0 — critical fix)
  - Trilinear interpolation for LUT application
  - Depth-weighted zone blending
- `src/hsl.rs` — HSL qualifier correction
  - Port from `run_fixed_hsl_lut_poc.py` functions `derive_hsl_corrections()` + `apply_hsl_corrections()`
  - 6 color qualifiers (Red/Skin, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, Magenta)
  - Per-qualifier: hue offset, saturation ratio, value offset
  - Soft cosine falloff qualifier masks, minimum saturation threshold 0.08
- `src/color.rs` — Color space conversions
  - RGB ↔ LAB (port from skimage implementation)
  - RGB ↔ HSV
  - D-Log M ↔ linear (port from `pipeline_utils.py`)
- `src/grading.rs` — Depth-aware ambiance grading (CPU fallback)
  - Port from `depth_aware_ambiance()` in POC scripts
  - Shadow lift, adaptive S-curve, highlight compression, local contrast, warmth, vibrance
  - All operations depth-graduated
- `src/calibration.rs` — Serialize/deserialize LUT + HSL params
  - Binary format: 5 LUTs (5 × 33³ × 3 × f32) + HSL corrections (6 × 3 floats) + metadata

### Crate: dorea-gpu
- `src/pipeline.rs` — wgpu compute pipeline setup
- `shaders/depth_lut.wgsl` — Trilinear LUT lookup with 5-zone depth blending
- `shaders/hsl_correct.wgsl` — 6-qualifier HSL correction
- `shaders/depth_grade.wgsl` — Full ambiance grading (shadow/contrast/highlight/warmth/vibrance)
- `shaders/lab_convert.wgsl` — RGB ↔ LAB for GPU
- Fused single-dispatch: LUT → HSL → grade in one GPU pass per frame
- Workgroup size 16×16, dispatch 240×135 for 4K

### Crate: dorea-video
- `src/decode.rs` — ffmpeg subprocess: video → raw frames (NVDEC)
- `src/encode.rs` — ffmpeg subprocess: raw frames → video (NVENC)
- `src/keyframes.rs` — Keyframe selection logic (interval-based)

## Phase 2: PyTorch Inference Integration

### Python module: python/dorea_inference/
- `vda.py` — Video Depth Anything inference
  - torch.compile optimized
  - Proxy resolution (512-1024px)
  - Temporal consistency mode for video
  - Output: 16-bit depth maps
- `raune.py` — RAUNE-Net inference
  - Load pretrained weights (141MB, 30 ResBlocks + CBAM)
  - Proxy resolution, keyframes only
  - Output: enhanced RGB frames
- `server.py` — Communication bridge
  - Shared memory or named pipes for frame data
  - JSON control messages for commands
  - Async pipeline: inference runs ahead of grading

### ONNX export utility
- `export_onnx.py` — Export RAUNE-Net to ONNX for benchmarking
- Compare PyTorch vs ONNX Runtime quality and speed
- Decision point: if ONNX quality matches, can eliminate Python dependency long-term

## Phase 3: End-to-End Video Pipeline

### Integration
- Wire up: decode → VDA depth → LUT+HSL+grade → encode
- Async frame pipeline: depth inference overlaps with grading
- Memory management: frame ring buffer, depth map ring buffer

### Calibration workflow
- `dorea calibrate`: process keyframes → build LUTs + HSL → save .dorea-cal file
- `dorea grade`: load .dorea-cal → process video frames → output video
- Can re-use calibration across clips from same dive (same water conditions)

### Benchmarks needed
- Proxy resolution quality: 512 vs 768 vs 1024px for VDA and RAUNE-Net
- 4K throughput: target 20-32 FPS
- VRAM usage: VDA + grading on RTX 3060 6GB
- Temporal consistency: verify no flicker on real dive clips

## Algorithm Reference (from POC)
All proven algorithms are in:
- `working/sea_thru_poc/run_fixed_hsl_lut_poc.py` — LUT build + HSL correction (BEST approach)
- `working/sea_thru_poc/run_depth_lut_amb_poc.py` — depth-stratified LUT + ambiance
- `working/sea_thru_poc/ambiance_grade.py` — core grading algorithms (LAB space)

## Key Constants
- LUT_SIZE = 33
- N_DEPTH_ZONES = 5
- EDGE_SCALE = 0.3, CONTRAST_SCALE = 0.3 (importance weighting)
- HSL qualifiers: 6 vectors with h_center/h_width as defined in POC
- Depth segments for grading: near warmth_a=6, far warmth_a=1 (graduated)
- Highlight knee = 0.88 (depth grading), 0.92 (final rolloff)
- Clarity sigma = 30px (local contrast gaussian)
